{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_fc38b5b6b5dc4bbd9ade45e7","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\taide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n6\t\n7\tCLI invocation: `adc`\n8\t\n9\t---\n10\t\n11\t## Problem Statement\n12\t\n13\tManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n14\t\n15\taide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## Core Architecture\n20\t\n21\t### The Hot Path\n22\t\n23\t```\n24\tUser utterance (voice or text)\n25\t  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n26\t  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms)\n27\t      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment}, ...]\n28\t  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n29\t      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n30\t      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s)\n31\t      output: {data, summary, urgency}\n32\t  → Results streamed via SSE to active canvas\n33\t  → Cards rendered client-side using component templates\n34\t```\n35\t\n36\tTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n37\t\n38\t### The Async Path\n39\t\n40\t```\n41\tTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n42\t  → NEEDLE task bead created (br create)\n43\t  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n44\t  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n45\t  → Result written to session store results table\n46\t  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n47\t```\n48\t\n49\t### The Self-Improvement Path\n50\t\n51\t```\n52\tFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n53\t  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n54\t      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML, component template)\n55\t      generates update\n56\t      surfaces diff to user\n57\t  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n58\t```\n59\t\n60\t---\n61\t\n62\t## Components\n63\t\n64\t### 1. Intent Router\n65\t\n66\t**Runtime:** Direct API call (haiku-class via ZAI proxy)\n67\t\n68\tOne LLM call per utterance. Receives the full utterance text, the project registry, and a segmentation prompt. Returns a JSON array of intent threads, each tagged with:\n69\t\n70\t```json\n71\t[\n72\t  {\n73\t    \"project_slug\": \"options-pipeline\",\n74\t    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n75\t    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n76\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n77\t  },\n78\t  {\n79\t    \"project_slug\": \"ibkr-mcp\",\n80\t    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n81\t    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n82\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"what's the state of the ibkr mcp\"\n83\t  }\n84\t]\n85\t```\n86\t\n87\tIntent types: `status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`\n88\t\n89\tThe router reads its segmentation prompt and the project registry from disk on each call — no caching. Hot-reload is automatic.\n90\t\n91\tAmbiguous intents: if confidence is below threshold, router returns an `intent_type: \"clarification\"` thread. The voice model or canvas handles the clarification round-trip before dispatching.\n92\t\n93\t### 2. Project Registry\n94\t\n95\t**Format:** YAML file, read per-invocation by the router\n96\t\n97\tDefines projects the router knows about:\n98\t\n99\t```yaml\n100\tprojects:\n101\t  options-pipeline:\n102\t    aliases: [\"the pipeline\", \"options\"]\n103\t    description: \"Options data pipeline on apexalgo-iad\"\n104\t    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n105\t    namespace: options\n106\t    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm]\n107\t    workflows:\n108\t      deploy:\n109\t        steps: [ci-status, image-tag, argocd-sync, pod-status]\n110\t\n111\t  ibkr-mcp:\n112\t    aliases: [\"ibkr\", \"the mcp\"]\n113\t    description: \"IBKR MCP server\"\n114\t    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n115\t    namespace: ibkr-mcp\n116\t    intent_support: [status, brainstorm, lookup]\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\tUpdated by the self-modification agent. Hot-reloaded on every router call.\n120\t\n121\t### 3. Fetch Strand\n122\t\n123\t**Runtime:** Deterministic code, no LLM\n124\t\n125\tExecutes a command matrix based on `intent_type` and the project's registry entry. No LLM decisions — the commands are determined by the intent type and project config.\n126\t\n127\tExample command matrix for `intent_type: status`:\n128\t\n129\t| Source | Command |\n130\t|--------|---------|\n131\t| Pod status | `kubectl get pods -n {namespace}` |\n132\t| ArgoCD sync | `curl argocd-ro-api/applications/{app}` |\n133\t| Git log | `git -C {repo_path} log -10 --oneline` |\n134\t| Bead list | `br list --project {slug} --status open` |\n135\t| CI status | `kubectl get workflows -n argo-workflows -l project={slug}` |\n136\t\n137\tResults are structured data passed to the Synthesize strand. Fetch runs each source concurrently; partial results (from sources that respond first) are passed to Synthesize incrementally to support streaming output.\n138\t\n139\tA `fetch_coverage` field tracks which sources succeeded and which failed. Failed sources are surfaced as caveats in the result.\n140\t\n141\t### 4. Synthesize Strand\n142\t\n143\t**Runtime:** Direct API call (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy)\n144\t\n145\tTakes `{intent_spec, fetched_context}` → produces `{data, summary}`.\n146\t\n147\t- `data`: structured JSON matching the component library's expected schema for this intent type\n148\t- `summary`: 1-3 sentence narration suitable for audio mode\n149\t\n150\tSingle-turn inference. Reads its system prompt from a file per invocation (hot-reload). The synthesize prompt is the highest-leverage artifact in the system — it defines result format, detail level, and what fields to include.\n151\t\n152\tRuns once per intent thread in parallel across all active threads.\n153\t\n154\t### 5. Escalate Strand\n155\t\n156\t**Runtime:** Direct API call + `br create`\n157\t\n158\tFor task-profile intents that need durable async handling:\n159\t1. One LLM call to formulate a bead body from the intent + context\n160\t2. `br create` to create the bead in the appropriate project\n161\t3. Returns a pending-card spec with the bead reference\n162\t\n163\tThe bead watcher subsequently bridges bead closure to result delivery.\n164\t\n165\t### 6. Voice Model\n166\t\n167\t**Runtime:** Realtime API (OpenAI or Claude Realtime, persistent session)\n168\t\n169\tThe conversational layer. Receives transcribed utterances (or audio directly), calls `dispatch_intent()` tool to trigger routing, and narrates results at appropriate moments.\n170\t\n171\tTool calls are triggers, not queries. `dispatch_intent()` returns an acknowledgment immediately; results arrive out-of-band and are surfaced by the voice model at natural conversational pauses.\n172\t\n173\tThe voice model's system prompt controls narration style, batching behavior, urgency handling, and multi-turn topic tracking. Read per session-turn from a file (hot-reload).\n174\t\n175\tIn audio mode there is no canvas. The voice model reads `result.summary` directly. When a user transitions from audio to canvas, pending results are rendered using the component library and appear on canvas — session continuity means the canvas catches up.\n176\t\n177\t### 7. UI-Regen Agent\n178\t\n179\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n180\t\n181\tSteward of the component library. Given a result's data shape:\n182\t1. Finds the best-fit existing component (semantic match against component descriptions)\n183\t2. If no good fit, generates a new component from scratch and stores it\n184\t3. Applies the component template to result data accounting for layout bucket\n185\t4. On feedback, iterates the component (updates library, triggers canvas re-render)\n186\t\n187\tMulti-step: reads library, generates or selects, writes back. Claude Code's file access and tool use make this the right runtime.\n188\t\n189\tCreated as a task bead; not on the hot path. Canvas updates in place when a component is versioned (SSE push: `component_updated: {component_id, version}`).\n190\t\n191\t### 8. Self-Modification Agent\n192\t\n193\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n194\t\n195\tReads and writes the artifacts that encode system behavior:\n196\t- Router prompt (`prompts/router.md`)\n197\t- Project registry (`config/registry.yaml`)\n198\t- Strand prompts (`prompts/fetch.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, etc.)\n199\t- Urgency classifier prompt (`prompts/urgency.md`)\n200\t- Voice model prompt (`prompts/voice.md`)\n201\t- Monitoring config (`config/monitoring.yaml`)\n202\t- Exception routing rules (`config/exceptions.yaml`)\n203\t\n204\tWorkflow for every modification:\n205\t1. Read current artifact\n206\t2. Understand intent of change\n207\t3. Generate updated artifact\n208\t4. Surface diff to user (in canvas or narrated in audio mode)\n209\t5. On approval: write artifact, hot-reload takes effect on next invocation\n210\t6. On rejection: discard\n211\t\n212\tSafety model:\n213\t- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\n214\t- All artifacts versioned; any update can be rolled back with one instruction\n215\t- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n216\t\n217\t### 9. Background Analysis Bead\n218\t\n219\t**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead, low priority)\n220\t\n221\tRuns on a schedule. Reads intent history and engagement signals from the session store, identifies patterns, proposes artifact updates.\n222\t\n223\tExamples:\n224\t- \"User consistently drills into status results for more detail → propose adding causality fields to synthesize prompt\"\n225\t- \"User ignores pipeline monitoring pushes for 5 days → propose lowering urgency tier\"\n226\t- \"After deploy status, user always asks about pod logs within 2 minutes → propose speculative pre-fetch\"\n227\t\n228\tProposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. Auto-apply only above a high confidence threshold.\n229\t\n230\t### 10. Bead Watcher\n231\t\n232\t**Runtime:** Daemon process, no LLM\n233\t\n234\tWatches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. On close:\n235\t1. Reads `session_id` from closed bead metadata\n236\t2. Looks up active surface in session store\n237\t3. Writes result to `results` table\n238\t4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\n239\t\n240\tPure I/O. Runs as a long-running process (Deployment, not a K8s Job per infrastructure conventions).\n241\t\n242\t---\n243\t\n244\t## Data Model\n245\t\n246\t### Session Store (SQLite)\n247\t\n248\t```sql\n249\tsessions (\n250\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n251\t  created_at  INTEGER,\n252\t  last_active INTEGER,\n253\t  primary_surface_id TEXT\n254\t)\n255\t\n256\tsurfaces (\n257\t  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n258\t  session_id      TEXT,\n259\t  type            TEXT,  -- 'canvas' | 'telegram' | 'audio'\n260\t  state           TEXT,  -- 'active' | 'idle' | 'disconnected'\n261\t  always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- 1 for Telegram\n262\t  last_seen       INTEGER\n263\t)\n264\t\n265\tutterances (\n266\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n267\t  session_id  TEXT,\n268\t  raw_text    TEXT,\n269\t  created_at  INTEGER\n270\t)\n271\t\n272\tintents (\n273\t  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n274\t  utterance_id TEXT,\n275\t  session_id   TEXT,\n276\t  topic_id     TEXT,\n277\t  project_slug TEXT,\n278\t  intent_type  TEXT,\n279\t  status       TEXT,  -- 'pending' | 'dispatched' | 'resolved' | 'cancelled'\n280\t  bead_ref     TEXT,  -- set for task-profile intents\n281\t  created_at   INTEGER,\n282\t  resolved_at  INTEGER\n283\t)\n284\t\n285\tresults (\n286\t  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n287\t  intent_id   TEXT,\n288\t  topic_id    TEXT,\n289\t  session_id  TEXT,\n290\t  summary     TEXT,\n291\t  data        TEXT,  -- JSON\n292\t  urgency     TEXT,  -- 'critical' | 'high' | 'normal' | 'low'\n293\t  created_at  INTEGER,\n294\t  surfaced_at INTEGER,\n295\t  acked_at    INTEGER\n296\t)\n297\t\n298\ttopics (\n299\t  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n300\t  label        TEXT,\n301\t  type         TEXT,  -- 'project' | 'research' | 'personal' | 'exception' | 'compound'\n302\t  project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n303\t  scope        TEXT,  -- 'session' | 'cross-session' | 'global'\n304\t  session_id   TEXT,  -- null for cross-session/global\n305\t  created_at   INTEGER,\n306\t  last_active  INTEGER,\n307\t  archived_at  INTEGER\n308\t)\n309\t\n310\ttopic_context_cache (\n311\t  topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n312\t  context_data TEXT,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n313\t  fetched_at  INTEGER,\n314\t  expires_at  INTEGER\n315\t)\n316\t\n317\tfeedback_signals (\n318\t  signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n319\t  signal_type  TEXT,\n320\t  session_id   TEXT,\n321\t  result_id    TEXT,\n322\t  topic_id     TEXT,\n323\t  timestamp    INTEGER,\n324\t  data         TEXT,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n325\t  surface_type TEXT,\n326\t  processed    INTEGER,  -- 0 or 1\n327\t  processed_at INTEGER\n328\t)\n329\t\n330\tintent_topics (\n331\t  intent_id TEXT,\n332\t  topic_id  TEXT,\n333\t  PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id)\n334\t)\n335\t```\n336\t\n337\t### Component Library (SQLite, separate DB)\n338\t\n339\t```sql\n340\tcomponents (\n341\t  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n342\t  name          TEXT,\n343\t  description   TEXT,\n344\t  html_template TEXT,\n345\t  version       INTEGER,\n346\t  created_at    INTEGER,\n347\t  last_used     INTEGER,\n348\t  usage_count   INTEGER\n349\t)\n350\t\n351\tcomponent_versions (\n352\t  component_id  TEXT,\n353\t  version       INTEGER,\n354\t  html_template TEXT,\n355\t  created_at    INTEGER,\n356\t  change_note   TEXT,\n357\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, version)\n358\t)\n359\t\n360\tcard_cache (\n361\t  result_id         TEXT,\n362\t  component_id      TEXT,\n363\t  component_version INTEGER,\n364\t  layout_bucket     TEXT,  -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\n365\t  rendered_html     TEXT,\n366\t  created_at        INTEGER,\n367\t  PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n368\t)\n369\t\n370\tcomponent_tags (\n371\t  component_id TEXT,\n372\t  tag TEXT,\n373\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, tag)\n374\t)\n375\t\n376\tcomponent_usage_patterns (\n377\t  component_id TEXT,\n378\t  result_type TEXT,  -- e.g., \"pod-status\", \"git-log\"\n379\t  match_score REAL,  -- 0-1\n380\t  sample_count INTEGER,\n381\t  last_matched INTEGER,\n382\t  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, result_type)\n383\t)\n384\t```\n385\t\n386\t---\n387\t\n388\t## File System Layout\n389\t\n390\t```\n391\taide-de-camp/\n392\t├── adc                      ← CLI entry point (shell script or Python package)\n393\t├── config/\n394\t│   ├── registry.yaml        ← project registry (hot-reloaded by router)\n395\t│   ├── monitoring.yaml      ← ambient monitoring rules\n396\t│   └── exceptions.yaml      ← exception routing rules\n397\t├── prompts/\n398\t│   ├── router.md            ← intent segmentation prompt\n399\t│   ├── synthesize.md        ← result generation prompt\n400\t│   ├── voice.md             ← voice model system prompt\n401\t│   ├── urgency.md           ← urgency classification prompt\n402\t│   ├── fetch/               ← per-intent-type fetch instructions\n403\t│   │   ├── status.md\n404\t│   │   ├── action.md\n405\t│   │   └── ...\n406\t│   └── escalate/            ← escalate/task-profile prompts\n407\t│       └── task-profile.md\n408\t├── src/\n409\t│   ├── main.py              ← FastAPI app entry point\n410\t│   ├── registry.py          ← project registry loader\n411\t│   ├── agents/              ← agent implementations\n412\t│   │   ├── self_modification.py  ← self-improvement agent\n413\t│   │   └── ui_regen.py          ← UI-regen agent\n414\t│   ├── components/          ← component library and hot-reload\n415\t│   │   ├── library.py           ← component library DB operations\n416\t│   │   └── hot_reload.py        ← hot-reload watcher\n417\t│   ├── context/             ← context warming and pre-fetch\n418\t│   │   ├── warmer.py            ← context warmer for active topics\n419\t│   │   └── prefetch.py          ← speculative pre-fetch\n420\t│   ├── conversation/        ← multi-turn conversation handling\n421\t│   ├── diff/                ← diff generation for results\n422\t│   ├── environment/         ← environment and repo discovery\n423\t│   │   └── discovery.py        ← repo scanner and registry\n424\t│   ├── escalate/            ← escalate strand for task-profile intents\n425\t│   │   ├── handler.py           ← escalate request handler\n426\t│   │   ├── llm.py               ← LLM calls for bead formulation\n427\t│   │   └── commands.py          ├── bead creation commands\n428\t│   ├── feedback/            ← feedback processing and background analysis\n429\t│   │   ├── processor.py         ← explicit feedback processor\n430\t│   │   ├── signals.py           ← implicit feedback signal tracking\n431\t│   │   └── background_analysis.py ← background analysis bead\n432\t│   ├── fetch/               ← fetch strand (deterministic, per intent type)\n433\t│   │   ├── commands.py          ← fetch command matrix\n434\t│   │   ├── executor.py          ← command executor\n435\t│   │   ├── orchestrator.py      ← parallel fetch orchestration\n436\t│   │   └── strand.py            ← fetch strand entry point\n437\t│   ├── intent/              ← intent router (LLM classification)\n438\t│   │   └── router.py            ← intent segmentation and routing\n439\t│   ├── memory/              ← memory store and extraction\n440\t│   │   ├── store.py             ← memory persistence\n441\t│   │   └── extraction.py        ← memory extraction from results\n442\t│   ├── monitoring/          ← ambient monitoring\n443\t│   │   └── ambient.py           ← ambient monitoring rules\n444\t│   ├── realtime/            ← OpenAI Realtime API voice session\n445\t│   │   ├── session.py           ← voice session handler\n446\t│   │   ├── batching.py          ← result batching for narration\n447\t│   │   ├── continuity.py        ← audio-to-canvas session continuity\n448\t│   │   └── dispatch.py          ← tool-as-trigger dispatch\n449\t│   ├── session/             ← session store (SQLite)\n450\t│   │   └── store.py             ← session store operations\n451\t│   ├── sse/                 ← SSE broadcasting\n452\t│   │   ├── broadcaster.py       ← SSE connection registry\n453\t│   │   └── events.py            ← SSE event types\n454\t│   ├── surface/             ← surface routing\n455\t│   │   └── router.py            ← result surface routing logic\n456\t│   ├── synthesize/          ← synthesize strand (LLM)\n457\t│   │   └── strand.py            ← result synthesis\n458\t│   ├── telegram/            ← Telegram fallback surface\n459\t│   │   └── fallback.py          ← Telegram delivery\n460\t│   ├── topic/               ← topic model\n461\t│   │   └── model.py             ← topic operations\n462\t│   ├── watcher/             ← bead watcher daemon\n463\t│   │   └── daemon.py            ← NEEDLE bead watcher\n464\t│   ├── canvas/              ← web frontend (SSE consumer, card renderer)\n465\t│   │   └── index.html           ← single-page canvas UI\n466\t│   └── cli/                 ← adc CLI\n467\t│       ├── main.py              ← CLI entry point\n468\t│       ├── commands.py          ← CLI commands\n469\t│       ├── config.py            ← CLI configuration\n470\t│       └── sse.py               ← SSE streaming for CLI\n471\t├── data/\n472\t│   ├── session.db           ← session store (SQLite)\n473\t│   └── components.db        ← component library (SQLite)\n474\t└── docs/\n475\t    ├── research/            ← architecture and design research\n476\t    ├── plan/\n477\t    │   └── plan.md          ← this file\n478\t    └── notes/\n479\t        └── naming.md\n480\t```\n481\t\n482\t---\n483\t\n484\t## Deployment Model\n485\t\n486\taide-de-camp is a **live web application**, not a static site. The FastAPI server is the intelligence layer — it handles SSE connections, maintains session state, runs the router and synthesize strands, and serves the frontend HTML. There is no CDN path (no CF Pages).\n487\t\n488\t### Why not static\n489\t\n490\t- SSE connections are long-lived; CDNs cannot proxy them\n491\t- WebSocket for the Realtime API voice session requires a persistent backend\n492\t- The session store (SQLite) and artifact store (prompts, registry) require a writable filesystem\n493\t- Hot-reload depends on the running server reading updated files from disk on each invocation\n494\t\n495\t### Current Deployment: Phase 0 (Hetzner server directly)\n496\t\n497\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n498\t\n499\tThe server runs as a process on the Hetzner server itself, not in k8s:\n500\t- NEEDLE workers and the aide-de-camp server share the same filesystem\n501\t- Self-modification agent writes directly to `prompts/` and `config/` — hot-reload works without any coordination\n502\t- Exposed via Tailscale (the server is already on the mesh); no ingress config needed\n503\t- SQLite DBs are local files; no PVC required\n504\t\n505\tRunning command: `uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000`\n506\t\n507\tNo container, no CI, no ArgoCD. Managed as a long-running process (see CLAUDE.md for restart commands).\n508\t\n509\t### Release flow (Phase 0)\n510\t\n511\tVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n512\t\n513\tRelease: `bump version in pyproject.toml` → `commit` → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → `push`.\n514\t\n515\t### Future: Phase 1+ (containerized, ardenone-cluster)\n516\t\n517\t**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n518\t\n519\tOnce session persistence and multi-surface routing are needed, containerize and move to k8s:\n520\t\n521\t```\n522\tDocker image: ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp\n523\tDeployment: ardenone-cluster, namespace: aide-de-camp\n524\tPVC: /data/ (SATA, ReadWriteOnce)\n525\t  /data/session.db        ← session store\n526\t  /data/components.db     ← component library\n527\t  /data/prompts/          ← hot-reloadable prompt files\n528\t  /data/config/           ← registry.yaml, monitoring.yaml, exceptions.yaml\n529\t```\n530\t\n531\tThe artifact store (prompts, registry) moves from the repo's working directory to the PVC. The self-modification agent updates artifacts via the aide-de-camp API (`PATCH /artifacts/{name}`), which writes to the PVC path the server reads from.\n532\t\n533\t### Future: Traefik configuration for SSE and WebSocket (Phase 1+)\n534\t\n535\t**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n536\t\n537\tTraefik's default response buffering breaks SSE. The IngressRoute needs:\n538\t\n539\t```yaml\n540\t# IngressRoute annotations\n541\ttraefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: aide-de-camp-strip-prefix@kubernetescrd\n542\t\n543\t# Middleware: disable buffering, extend timeouts\n544\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n545\tkind: Middleware\n546\tmetadata:\n547\t  name: aide-de-camp-streaming\n548\tspec:\n549\t  buffering:\n550\t    maxResponseBodyBytes: 0   # disable buffering\n551\t  headers:\n552\t    customResponseHeaders:\n553\t      X-Accel-Buffering: \"no\"\n554\t```\n555\t\n556\tServersTransport timeout:\n557\t\n558\t```yaml\n559\tapiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\n560\tkind: ServersTransport\n561\tmetadata:\n562\t  name: aide-de-camp-longlived\n563\tspec:\n564\t  forwardingTimeouts:\n565\t    responseHeaderTimeout: 0    # no timeout on SSE streams\n566\t    dialTimeout: 5s\n567\t```\n568\t\n569\tDUCK-E's existing WebSocket IngressRoute config is the reference implementation for this cluster.\n570\t\n571\t### SQLite concurrency\n572\t\n573\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n574\t\n575\tMultiple writers (bead watcher, background analysis bead, session handler) require WAL mode to avoid lock contention:\n576\t\n577\t```python\n578\t# On DB open\n579\tconn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n580\tconn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n581\t```\n582\t\n583\tWAL mode allows concurrent reads during writes and serializes multiple writers without blocking. For a personal single-user app, this is sufficient.\n584\t\n585\t### adc CLI connectivity\n586\t\n587\tThe `adc` CLI is a thin HTTP client. It talks to the running server over Tailscale:\n588\t\n589\t```bash\n590\t# ~/.config/adc/config\n591\tserver_url = \"http://localhost:8000\"   # Phase 0 (local)\n592\t# Future: \"http://aide-de-camp.ardenone.com\" (Phase 1+)\n593\t```\n594\t\n595\tNo local inference. The CLI sends requests to the FastAPI backend and streams the SSE response to the terminal.\n596\t\n597\t### Future: CI/CD (Phase 1+)\n598\t\n599\t**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n600\t\n601\tNew Argo WorkflowTemplate `aide-de-camp-build` on iad-ci:\n602\t- Docker build → `ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp`\n603\t- Update image tag in `jedarden/declarative-config` (k8s/ardenone-cluster/aide-de-camp/)\n604\t- ArgoCD syncs automatically\n605\t\n606\tSame pattern as every other containerized service in the stack.\n607\t\n608\t---\n609\t\n610\t## Technology Stack\n611\t\n612\t| Layer | Choice | Reason |\n613\t|-------|--------|--------|\n614\t| Backend | FastAPI (Python) | Reuse DUCK-E scaffolding; SSE + WebSocket support; async-native |\n615\t| STT | Web Speech API → whisper-stt fallback | Zero-latency browser-native; whisper-stt already deployed |\n616\t| Voice session | OpenAI Realtime API (via DUCK-E session handler) | DUCK-E scaffolding already built on this |\n617\t| LLM calls | ZAI proxy (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) | Pay-per-token, no subscription pressure on hot path |\n618\t| Task workers | NEEDLE + Claude Code | Existing infrastructure, unchanged |\n619\t| Session store | SQLite (WAL mode) | Lightweight, local, additive-only schema; single-user app |\n620\t| Artifact store | PVC-mounted filesystem (Phase 1+) / local filesystem (Phase 0) | Writable at runtime; hot-reload reads per-invocation |\n621\t| Frontend | Vanilla JS + Web Components | No build pipeline; SSE consumer + client-side card renderer |\n622\t| Phase 0 hosting | Hetzner server, local process | Simplest path; shared filesystem with NEEDLE workers |\n623\t| Phase 1+ hosting | ardenone-cluster, k8s Deployment | Behind existing Traefik + Tailscale ingress |\n624\t\n625\t---\n626\t\n627\t## Implementation Phases\n628\t\n629\t### Phase 0 — Minimal Viable Surface (~2 days)\n630\t\n631\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n632\t\n633\tValidates the core question: does routing + parallel dispatch reduce friction?\n634\t\n635\t- Single HTML page: textarea + mic button (Web Speech API)\n636\t- `POST /dispatch` endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream\n637\t- Results appear as basic cards as agents resolve\n638\t- No persistence, no auth, no component library, no ambient push\n639\t\n640\tDeliverable: the core query loop working end-to-end.\n641\t\n642\t### Phase 1 — Session and Topics (~1 week)\n643\t\n644\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n645\t\n646\tResults persist; the canvas has memory.\n647\t\n648\t- Session store (SQLite, 7+ tables with topic_context_cache and feedback_signals)\n649\t- Topic model: canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place\n650\t- Telegram surface fallback (reuse telegram-claude-bridge)\n651\t- Bead watcher: closed NEEDLE beads push results to active surface\n652\t- Workload summary on reconnect\n653\t- Staleness indicators on cards\n654\t\n655\tDeliverable: sessions that survive browser refresh; Telegram fallback working.\n656\t\n657\t### Phase 2 — Self-Improvement Loop (~2 weeks)\n658\t\n659\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n660\t\n661\tThe interface can be improved by talking into it.\n662\t\n663\t- Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n664\t- Hot-reload confirmed at every artifact layer\n665\t- Component library: UI-regen agent generates first components from actual result shapes\n666\t- Canvas live-updates when a component is versioned\n667\t- Explicit feedback processed: \"always include X\" → prompt updated → hot-reloaded\n668\t\n669\tDeliverable: at least one end-to-end self-modification cycle working (user instructs change, diff surfaced, approved, takes effect without redeploy).\n670\t\n671\t### Phase 3 — Responsiveness (~2-3 weeks)\n672\t\n673\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n674\t\n675\tThe interface feels alive, not just reactive.\n676\t\n677\t- Ambient monitoring: active topics watched for state changes\n678\t- Diff-aware results: show what changed since last result\n679\t- Pre-warmed context: active topics refreshed in background every N minutes\n680\t- Multi-turn within topic: follow-up questions deepen current topic context\n681\t- Speculative pre-fetch: common follow-up patterns pre-fetched\n682\t- Notification batching in audio mode\n683\t- Implicit feedback signals fed to background analysis bead\n684\t\n685\tDeliverable: monitoring fires unprompted for a watched topic; follow-up questions are visibly faster.\n686\t\n687\t### Phase 4 — Audio Surface (~1-2 weeks)\n688\t\n689\t**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n690\t\n691\tFull audio mode via Realtime API.\n692\t\n693\t- Realtime API voice session replaces text input path\n694\t- Tool-as-trigger model: `dispatch_intent()` returns ack, result arrives async\n695\t- Urgency-tiered voice narration\n696\t- Audio-to-canvas session continuity\n697\t\n698\tDeliverable: full voice session with canvas catch-up on surface switch.\n699\t\n700\t### Future Work\n701\t\n702\t**Status: NOT STARTED** ❌\n703\t\n704\tPotential enhancements beyond Phase 4:\n705\t- Multi-modal input (image processing for UI feedback via Agentation)\n706\t- Advanced topic clustering and auto-archival\n707\t- Cross-session context persistence with summarization\n708\t- Mobile-native surface (iOS/Android app)\n709\t- Collaborative sessions (multi-user shared canvases)\n710\t- Advanced memory extraction with semantic search\n711\t\n712\t---\n713\t\n714\t## Self-Improvement: Hot-Reload Architecture\n715\t\n716\tEvery artifact that encodes behavior is readable, writable, and reloaded per-invocation:\n717\t\n718\t| Behavior | Artifact | Reload point |\n719\t|----------|----------|-------------|\n720\t| Utterance segmentation | `prompts/router.md` | Each router call |\n721\t| Intent→project routing | `config/registry.yaml` | Each router call |\n722\t| Context fetch strategy | `prompts/fetch/{type}.md` | Each fetch invocation |\n723\t| Result format and detail | `prompts/synthesize.md` | Each synthesize call |\n724\t| Voice narration style | `prompts/voice.md` | Each session turn |\n725\t| Urgency classification | `prompts/urgency.md` | Each escalate call |\n726\t| Visual rendering | Component library (DB) | Each card render |\n727\t| Monitoring rules | `config/monitoring.yaml` | Each monitoring tick |\n728\t\n729\tPer-invocation reload is the implementation strategy (read file, check mtime). File watching adds complexity for no benefit — prompts change rarely and file reads are cheap.\n730\t\n731\t---\n732\t\n733\t## Surface Routing Rules\n734\t\n735\tWhen a result is ready to surface, the bead watcher or synthesize strand uses this priority:\n736\t\n737\t1. The surface the utterance originated from (if still connected)\n738\t2. Most recently active connected surface\n739\t3. Any connected surface\n740\t4. Telegram (always-available fallback)\n741\t\n742\tException-class results (urgency: critical, type: exception) push to Telegram regardless of canvas state if no canvas has been active within the past N minutes.\n743\t\n744\t---\n745\t\n746\t## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n747\t\n748\taide-de-camp adds a routing and rendering layer on top of existing infrastructure without replacing any of it:\n749\t\n750\t- **NEEDLE workers** — unchanged. Task beads work exactly as before. The bead watcher is a new daemon that observes bead closure events.\n751\t- **telegram-claude-bridge** — reused for Telegram surface and HUMAN bead push delivery.\n752\t- **whisper-stt** — already deployed on ardenone-cluster; available as STT backend.\n753\t- **ZAI proxy** — all direct API calls (router, synthesize, escalate) route through `llm-proxy.ardenone.com`.\n754\t- **claude-governor** — governs subscription token consumption for self-modification and UI-regen task beads.\n755\t- **DUCK-E** — FastAPI scaffolding, WebSocket handling, OpenAI Realtime API session management, and middleware reused as the voice layer foundation.\n756\t- **beads (br CLI)** — task work items and HUMAN exceptions only. Conversational session state lives in a separate SQLite session store.\n757\t- **kubectl proxies** — fetch strand uses existing kubectl proxy access per cluster.\n758\t- **ArgoCD read-only proxy** — fetch strand reads ArgoCD application state via `argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444`.\n759\t\n760\tNet-new code: aide-de-camp is a substantial implementation spanning multiple subsystems. See the File System Layout section for the full module breakdown.\n761\t\n762\t---\n763\t\n764\t## Security Model\n765\t\n766\t- aide-de-camp runs on the Hetzner server; all cluster access via read-only kubectl proxies (same as existing tooling)\n767\t- No cluster credentials stored by aide-de-camp; existing proxy infrastructure holds them\n768\t- Self-modification agent writes only to the `prompts/` and `config/` directories within the repo\n769\t- All artifact changes go through diff-review before application\n770\t- Rollback available for any artifact via version history\n771\t\n772\t---\n773\t\n774\t## Open Questions\n775\t\n776\t1. **Routing accuracy** — how reliably can a haiku-class model split a rambling multi-project utterance into clean tagged threads? What's the false-split / under-split rate in practice?\n777\t\n778\t2. **Context latency** — can the fetch strand reliably hit kubectl + ArgoCD + git + beads within a 2-3s window? Which sources need timeouts and what's the degraded-result behavior?\n779\t\n780\t3. **Component selection** — when does the UI-regen agent generate a new component vs. stretch an existing one? How is \"good enough\" match defined?\n781\t\n782\t4. **Concurrency budget** — how many parallel synthesize calls can the ZAI proxy handle without queue pressure affecting the <3s target?\n783\t\n784\t5. **Topic vague reference resolution** — \"the pipeline\" vs. \"options pipeline\" vs. \"options-pipeline\". When does the router resolve from context vs. ask for clarification?\n785\t\n786\t6. **Voice UX** — push-to-talk vs. continuous listening + VAD silence detection. What's the right default for the audio surface?\n787\t\n788\t7. **Disambiguation flow** — when router confidence is below threshold, how does the clarification round-trip work without breaking conversational flow in audio mode?\n789\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3716d983-f33e-48e2-a75f-c775ebb51d55","uuid":"6989ad4f-763e-4001-980b-a5535d718284","timestamp":"2026-07-02T23:22:04.049Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n\n## Overview\n\naide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n\nCLI invocation: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## Problem Statement\n\nManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead. The same problem extends beyond coding: research, lookups, personal context, reminders — anything that requires knowing which system to ask before you can ask it.\n\naide-de-camp eliminates that overhead by providing a single input surface that routes automatically, dispatches in parallel, and returns organized results without requiring the user to know where to ask.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architecture\n\n### The Hot Path\n\n```\nUser utterance (voice or text)\n  → STT transcription (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n  → Intent Router (one LLM call, haiku-class, ~500ms)\n      output: [{project_slug, intent_type, urgency, utterance_fragment}, ...]\n  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize workers (one per intent thread)\n      Fetch: deterministic code, executes command matrix based on intent_type\n      Synthesize: one LLM call per thread (sonnet-class, ~1-2s)\n      output: {data, summary, urgency}\n  → Results streamed via SSE to active canvas\n  → Cards rendered client-side using component templates\n```\n\nTarget: < 3 seconds from utterance to first partial result on canvas. No Claude Code session startup on this path.\n\n### The Async Path\n\n```\nTask-profile intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n  → NEEDLE task bead created (br create)\n  → Existing Claude Code workers pick up bead normally\n  → Bead watcher detects closure event\n  → Result written to session store results table\n  → SSE push to active canvas (or Telegram if no canvas)\n```\n\n### The Self-Improvement Path\n\n```\nFeedback signal (explicit instruction or implicit engagement pattern)\n  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE task bead)\n      reads target artifact (prompt file, registry YAML, component template)\n      generates update\n      surfaces diff to user\n  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded on next invocation\n```\n\n---\n\n## Components\n\n### 1. Intent Router\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call (haiku-class via ZAI proxy)\n\nOne LLM call per utterance. Receives the full utterance text, the project registry, and a segmentation prompt. Returns a JSON array of intent threads, each tagged with:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"options-pipeline\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"project_slug\": \"ibkr-mcp\",\n    \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n    \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"what's the state of the ibkr mcp\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\nIntent types: `status`, `action`, `brainstorm`, `lookup`, `reminder`, `self-modification`, `monitoring-config`, `task-profile`, `clarification`\n\nThe router reads its segmentation prompt and the project registry from disk on each call — no caching. Hot-reload is automatic.\n\nAmbiguous intents: if confidence is below threshold, router returns an `intent_type: \"clarification\"` thread. The voice model or canvas handles the clarification round-trip before dispatching.\n\n### 2. Project Registry\n\n**Format:** YAML file, read per-invocation by the router\n\nDefines projects the router knows about:\n\n```yaml\nprojects:\n  options-pipeline:\n    aliases: [\"the pipeline\", \"options\"]\n    description: \"Options data pipeline on apexalgo-iad\"\n    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n    namespace: options\n    intent_support: [status, action, brainstorm]\n    workflows:\n      deploy:\n        steps: [ci-status, image-tag, argocd-sync, pod-status]\n\n  ibkr-mcp:\n    aliases: [\"ibkr\", \"the mcp\"]\n    description: \"IBKR MCP server\"\n    cluster: apexalgo-iad\n    namespace: ibkr-mcp\n    intent_support: [status, brainstorm, lookup]\n```\n\nUpdated by the self-modification agent. Hot-reloaded on every router call.\n\n### 3. Fetch Strand\n\n**Runtime:** Deterministic code, no LLM\n\nExecutes a command matrix based on `intent_type` and the project's registry entry. No LLM decisions — the commands are determined by the intent type and project config.\n\nExample command matrix for `intent_type: status`:\n\n| Source | Command |\n|--------|---------|\n| Pod status | `kubectl get pods -n {namespace}` |\n| ArgoCD sync | `curl argocd-ro-api/applications/{app}` |\n| Git log | `git -C {repo_path} log -10 --oneline` |\n| Bead list | `br list --project {slug} --status open` |\n| CI status | `kubectl get workflows -n argo-workflows -l project={slug}` |\n\nResults are structured data passed to the Synthesize strand. Fetch runs each source concurrently; partial results (from sources that respond first) are passed to Synthesize incrementally to support streaming output.\n\nA `fetch_coverage` field tracks which sources succeeded and which failed. Failed sources are surfaced as caveats in the result.\n\n### 4. Synthesize Strand\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy)\n\nTakes `{intent_spec, fetched_context}` → produces `{data, summary}`.\n\n- `data`: structured JSON matching the component library's expected schema for this intent type\n- `summary`: 1-3 sentence narration suitable for audio mode\n\nSingle-turn inference. Reads its system prompt from a file per invocation (hot-reload). The synthesize prompt is the highest-leverage artifact in the system — it defines result format, detail level, and what fields to include.\n\nRuns once per intent thread in parallel across all active threads.\n\n### 5. Escalate Strand\n\n**Runtime:** Direct API call + `br create`\n\nFor task-profile intents that need durable async handling:\n1. One LLM call to formulate a bead body from the intent + context\n2. `br create` to create the bead in the appropriate project\n3. Returns a pending-card spec with the bead reference\n\nThe bead watcher subsequently bridges bead closure to result delivery.\n\n### 6. Voice Model\n\n**Runtime:** Realtime API (OpenAI or Claude Realtime, persistent session)\n\nThe conversational layer. Receives transcribed utterances (or audio directly), calls `dispatch_intent()` tool to trigger routing, and narrates results at appropriate moments.\n\nTool calls are triggers, not queries. `dispatch_intent()` returns an acknowledgment immediately; results arrive out-of-band and are surfaced by the voice model at natural conversational pauses.\n\nThe voice model's system prompt controls narration style, batching behavior, urgency handling, and multi-turn topic tracking. Read per session-turn from a file (hot-reload).\n\nIn audio mode there is no canvas. The voice model reads `result.summary` directly. When a user transitions from audio to canvas, pending results are rendered using the component library and appear on canvas — session continuity means the canvas catches up.\n\n### 7. UI-Regen Agent\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n\nSteward of the component library. Given a result's data shape:\n1. Finds the best-fit existing component (semantic match against component descriptions)\n2. If no good fit, generates a new component from scratch and stores it\n3. Applies the component template to result data accounting for layout bucket\n4. On feedback, iterates the component (updates library, triggers canvas re-render)\n\nMulti-step: reads library, generates or selects, writes back. Claude Code's file access and tool use make this the right runtime.\n\nCreated as a task bead; not on the hot path. Canvas updates in place when a component is versioned (SSE push: `component_updated: {component_id, version}`).\n\n### 8. Self-Modification Agent\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead)\n\nReads and writes the artifacts that encode system behavior:\n- Router prompt (`prompts/router.md`)\n- Project registry (`config/registry.yaml`)\n- Strand prompts (`prompts/fetch.md`, `prompts/synthesize.md`, etc.)\n- Urgency classifier prompt (`prompts/urgency.md`)\n- Voice model prompt (`prompts/voice.md`)\n- Monitoring config (`config/monitoring.yaml`)\n- Exception routing rules (`config/exceptions.yaml`)\n\nWorkflow for every modification:\n1. Read current artifact\n2. Understand intent of change\n3. Generate updated artifact\n4. Surface diff to user (in canvas or narrated in audio mode)\n5. On approval: write artifact, hot-reload takes effect on next invocation\n6. On rejection: discard\n\nSafety model:\n- Diffs before every application — no silent changes\n- All artifacts versioned; any update can be rolled back with one instruction\n- Confidence threshold: unambiguous changes (adding an alias) can auto-apply with notification; structural changes always require explicit approval\n\n### 9. Background Analysis Bead\n\n**Runtime:** Claude Code via NEEDLE (task bead, low priority)\n\nRuns on a schedule. Reads intent history and engagement signals from the session store, identifies patterns, proposes artifact updates.\n\nExamples:\n- \"User consistently drills into status results for more detail → propose adding causality fields to synthesize prompt\"\n- \"User ignores pipeline monitoring pushes for 5 days → propose lowering urgency tier\"\n- \"After deploy status, user always asks about pod logs within 2 minutes → propose speculative pre-fetch\"\n\nProposals are surfaced as cards on canvas for user review. Auto-apply only above a high confidence threshold.\n\n### 10. Bead Watcher\n\n**Runtime:** Daemon process, no LLM\n\nWatches for bead close events from NEEDLE workers. On close:\n1. Reads `session_id` from closed bead metadata\n2. Looks up active surface in session store\n3. Writes result to `results` table\n4. Fires SSE push to canvas or Telegram push if no canvas active\n\nPure I/O. Runs as a long-running process (Deployment, not a K8s Job per infrastructure conventions).\n\n---\n\n## Data Model\n\n### Session Store (SQLite)\n\n```sql\nsessions (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  created_at  INTEGER,\n  last_active INTEGER,\n  primary_surface_id TEXT\n)\n\nsurfaces (\n  id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  session_id      TEXT,\n  type            TEXT,  -- 'canvas' | 'telegram' | 'audio'\n  state           TEXT,  -- 'active' | 'idle' | 'disconnected'\n  always_available INTEGER DEFAULT 0,  -- 1 for Telegram\n  last_seen       INTEGER\n)\n\nutterances (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  session_id  TEXT,\n  raw_text    TEXT,\n  created_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nintents (\n  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  utterance_id TEXT,\n  session_id   TEXT,\n  topic_id     TEXT,\n  project_slug TEXT,\n  intent_type  TEXT,\n  status       TEXT,  -- 'pending' | 'dispatched' | 'resolved' | 'cancelled'\n  bead_ref     TEXT,  -- set for task-profile intents\n  created_at   INTEGER,\n  resolved_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nresults (\n  id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  intent_id   TEXT,\n  topic_id    TEXT,\n  session_id  TEXT,\n  summary     TEXT,\n  data        TEXT,  -- JSON\n  urgency     TEXT,  -- 'critical' | 'high' | 'normal' | 'low'\n  created_at  INTEGER,\n  surfaced_at INTEGER,\n  acked_at    INTEGER\n)\n\ntopics (\n  id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  label        TEXT,\n  type         TEXT,  -- 'project' | 'research' | 'personal' | 'exception' | 'compound'\n  project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n  scope        TEXT,  -- 'session' | 'cross-session' | 'global'\n  session_id   TEXT,  -- null for cross-session/global\n  created_at   INTEGER,\n  last_active  INTEGER,\n  archived_at  INTEGER\n)\n\ntopic_context_cache (\n  topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  context_data TEXT,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n  fetched_at  INTEGER,\n  expires_at  INTEGER\n)\n\nfeedback_signals (\n  signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  signal_type  TEXT,\n  session_id   TEXT,\n  result_id    TEXT,\n  topic_id     TEXT,\n  timestamp    INTEGER,\n  data         TEXT,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n  surface_type TEXT,\n  processed    INTEGER,  -- 0 or 1\n  processed_at INTEGER\n)\n\nintent_topics (\n  intent_id TEXT,\n  topic_id  TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id)\n)\n```\n\n### Component Library (SQLite, separate DB)\n\n```sql\ncomponents (\n  id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n  name          TEXT,\n  description   TEXT,\n  html_template TEXT,\n  version       INTEGER,\n  created_at    INTEGER,\n  last_used     INTEGER,\n  usage_count   INTEGER\n)\n\ncomponent_versions (\n  component_id  TEXT,\n  version       INTEGER,\n  html_template TEXT,\n  created_at    INTEGER,\n  change_note   TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, version)\n)\n\ncard_cache (\n  result_id         TEXT,\n  component_id      TEXT,\n  component_version INTEGER,\n  layout_bucket     TEXT,  -- 'compact' | 'normal' | 'expanded'\n  rendered_html     TEXT,\n  created_at        INTEGER,\n  PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n)\n\ncomponent_tags (\n  component_id TEXT,\n  tag TEXT,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, tag)\n)\n\ncomponent_usage_patterns (\n  component_id TEXT,\n  result_type TEXT,  -- e.g., \"pod-status\", \"git-log\"\n  match_score REAL,  -- 0-1\n  sample_count INTEGER,\n  last_matched INTEGER,\n  PRIMARY KEY (component_id, result_type)\n)\n```\n\n---\n\n## File System Layout\n\n```\naide-de-camp/\n├── adc                      ← CLI entry point (shell script or Python package)\n├── config/\n│   ├── registry.yaml        ← project registry (hot-reloaded by router)\n│   ├── monitoring.yaml      ← ambient monitoring rules\n│   └── exceptions.yaml      ← exception routing rules\n├── prompts/\n│   ├── router.md            ← intent segmentation prompt\n│   ├── synthesize.md        ← result generation prompt\n│   ├── voice.md             ← voice model system prompt\n│   ├── urgency.md           ← urgency classification prompt\n│   ├── fetch/               ← per-intent-type fetch instructions\n│   │   ├── status.md\n│   │   ├── action.md\n│   │   └── ...\n│   └── escalate/            ← escalate/task-profile prompts\n│       └── task-profile.md\n├── src/\n│   ├── main.py              ← FastAPI app entry point\n│   ├── registry.py          ← project registry loader\n│   ├── agents/              ← agent implementations\n│   │   ├── self_modification.py  ← self-improvement agent\n│   │   └── ui_regen.py          ← UI-regen agent\n│   ├── components/          ← component library and hot-reload\n│   │   ├── library.py           ← component library DB operations\n│   │   └── hot_reload.py        ← hot-reload watcher\n│   ├── context/             ← context warming and pre-fetch\n│   │   ├── warmer.py            ← context warmer for active topics\n│   │   └── prefetch.py          ← speculative pre-fetch\n│   ├── conversation/        ← multi-turn conversation handling\n│   ├── diff/                ← diff generation for results\n│   ├── environment/         ← environment and repo discovery\n│   │   └── discovery.py        ← repo scanner and registry\n│   ├── escalate/            ← escalate strand for task-profile intents\n│   │   ├── handler.py           ← escalate request handler\n│   │   ├── llm.py               ← LLM calls for bead formulation\n│   │   └── commands.py          ├── bead creation commands\n│   ├── feedback/            ← feedback processing and background analysis\n│   │   ├── processor.py         ← explicit feedback processor\n│   │   ├── signals.py           ← implicit feedback signal tracking\n│   │   └── background_analysis.py ← background analysis bead\n│   ├── fetch/               ← fetch strand (deterministic, per intent type)\n│   │   ├── commands.py          ← fetch command matrix\n│   │   ├── executor.py          ← command executor\n│   │   ├── orchestrator.py      ← parallel fetch orchestration\n│   │   └── strand.py            ← fetch strand entry point\n│   ├── intent/              ← intent router (LLM classification)\n│   │   └── router.py            ← intent segmentation and routing\n│   ├── memory/              ← memory store and extraction\n│   │   ├── store.py             ← memory persistence\n│   │   └── extraction.py        ← memory extraction from results\n│   ├── monitoring/          ← ambient monitoring\n│   │   └── ambient.py           ← ambient monitoring rules\n│   ├── realtime/            ← OpenAI Realtime API voice session\n│   │   ├── session.py           ← voice session handler\n│   │   ├── batching.py          ← result batching for narration\n│   │   ├── continuity.py        ← audio-to-canvas session continuity\n│   │   └── dispatch.py          ← tool-as-trigger dispatch\n│   ├── session/             ← session store (SQLite)\n│   │   └── store.py             ← session store operations\n│   ├── sse/                 ← SSE broadcasting\n│   │   ├── broadcaster.py       ← SSE connection registry\n│   │   └── events.py            ← SSE event types\n│   ├── surface/             ← surface routing\n│   │   └── router.py            ← result surface routing logic\n│   ├── synthesize/          ← synthesize strand (LLM)\n│   │   └── strand.py            ← result synthesis\n│   ├── telegram/            ← Telegram fallback surface\n│   │   └── fallback.py          ← Telegram delivery\n│   ├── topic/               ← topic model\n│   │   └── model.py             ← topic operations\n│   ├── watcher/             ← bead watcher daemon\n│   │   └── daemon.py            ← NEEDLE bead watcher\n│   ├── canvas/              ← web frontend (SSE consumer, card renderer)\n│   │   └── index.html           ← single-page canvas UI\n│   └── cli/                 ← adc CLI\n│       ├── main.py              ← CLI entry point\n│       ├── commands.py          ← CLI commands\n│       ├── config.py            ← CLI configuration\n│       └── sse.py               ← SSE streaming for CLI\n├── data/\n│   ├── session.db           ← session store (SQLite)\n│   └── components.db        ← component library (SQLite)\n└── docs/\n    ├── research/            ← architecture and design research\n    ├── plan/\n    │   └── plan.md          ← this file\n    └── notes/\n        └── naming.md\n```\n\n---\n\n## Deployment Model\n\naide-de-camp is a **live web application**, not a static site. The FastAPI server is the intelligence layer — it handles SSE connections, maintains session state, runs the router and synthesize strands, and serves the frontend HTML. There is no CDN path (no CF Pages).\n\n### Why not static\n\n- SSE connections are long-lived; CDNs cannot proxy them\n- WebSocket for the Realtime API voice session requires a persistent backend\n- The session store (SQLite) and artifact store (prompts, registry) require a writable filesystem\n- Hot-reload depends on the running server reading updated files from disk on each invocation\n\n### Current Deployment: Phase 0 (Hetzner server directly)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nThe server runs as a process on the Hetzner server itself, not in k8s:\n- NEEDLE workers and the aide-de-camp server share the same filesystem\n- Self-modification agent writes directly to `prompts/` and `config/` — hot-reload works without any coordination\n- Exposed via Tailscale (the server is already on the mesh); no ingress config needed\n- SQLite DBs are local files; no PVC required\n\nRunning command: `uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000`\n\nNo container, no CI, no ArgoCD. Managed as a long-running process (see CLAUDE.md for restart commands).\n\n### Release flow (Phase 0)\n\nVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n\nRelease: `bump version in pyproject.toml` → `commit` → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → `push`.\n\n### Future: Phase 1+ (containerized, ardenone-cluster)\n\n**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n\nOnce session persistence and multi-surface routing are needed, containerize and move to k8s:\n\n```\nDocker image: ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp\nDeployment: ardenone-cluster, namespace: aide-de-camp\nPVC: /data/ (SATA, ReadWriteOnce)\n  /data/session.db        ← session store\n  /data/components.db     ← component library\n  /data/prompts/          ← hot-reloadable prompt files\n  /data/config/           ← registry.yaml, monitoring.yaml, exceptions.yaml\n```\n\nThe artifact store (prompts, registry) moves from the repo's working directory to the PVC. The self-modification agent updates artifacts via the aide-de-camp API (`PATCH /artifacts/{name}`), which writes to the PVC path the server reads from.\n\n### Future: Traefik configuration for SSE and WebSocket (Phase 1+)\n\n**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n\nTraefik's default response buffering breaks SSE. The IngressRoute needs:\n\n```yaml\n# IngressRoute annotations\ntraefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: aide-de-camp-strip-prefix@kubernetescrd\n\n# Middleware: disable buffering, extend timeouts\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Middleware\nmetadata:\n  name: aide-de-camp-streaming\nspec:\n  buffering:\n    maxResponseBodyBytes: 0   # disable buffering\n  headers:\n    customResponseHeaders:\n      X-Accel-Buffering: \"no\"\n```\n\nServersTransport timeout:\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: ServersTransport\nmetadata:\n  name: aide-de-camp-longlived\nspec:\n  forwardingTimeouts:\n    responseHeaderTimeout: 0    # no timeout on SSE streams\n    dialTimeout: 5s\n```\n\nDUCK-E's existing WebSocket IngressRoute config is the reference implementation for this cluster.\n\n### SQLite concurrency\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nMultiple writers (bead watcher, background analysis bead, session handler) require WAL mode to avoid lock contention:\n\n```python\n# On DB open\nconn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\nconn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n```\n\nWAL mode allows concurrent reads during writes and serializes multiple writers without blocking. For a personal single-user app, this is sufficient.\n\n### adc CLI connectivity\n\nThe `adc` CLI is a thin HTTP client. It talks to the running server over Tailscale:\n\n```bash\n# ~/.config/adc/config\nserver_url = \"http://localhost:8000\"   # Phase 0 (local)\n# Future: \"http://aide-de-camp.ardenone.com\" (Phase 1+)\n```\n\nNo local inference. The CLI sends requests to the FastAPI backend and streams the SSE response to the terminal.\n\n### Future: CI/CD (Phase 1+)\n\n**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n\nNew Argo WorkflowTemplate `aide-de-camp-build` on iad-ci:\n- Docker build → `ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp`\n- Update image tag in `jedarden/declarative-config` (k8s/ardenone-cluster/aide-de-camp/)\n- ArgoCD syncs automatically\n\nSame pattern as every other containerized service in the stack.\n\n---\n\n## Technology Stack\n\n| Layer | Choice | Reason |\n|-------|--------|--------|\n| Backend | FastAPI (Python) | Reuse DUCK-E scaffolding; SSE + WebSocket support; async-native |\n| STT | Web Speech API → whisper-stt fallback | Zero-latency browser-native; whisper-stt already deployed |\n| Voice session | OpenAI Realtime API (via DUCK-E session handler) | DUCK-E scaffolding already built on this |\n| LLM calls | ZAI proxy (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) | Pay-per-token, no subscription pressure on hot path |\n| Task workers | NEEDLE + Claude Code | Existing infrastructure, unchanged |\n| Session store | SQLite (WAL mode) | Lightweight, local, additive-only schema; single-user app |\n| Artifact store | PVC-mounted filesystem (Phase 1+) / local filesystem (Phase 0) | Writable at runtime; hot-reload reads per-invocation |\n| Frontend | Vanilla JS + Web Components | No build pipeline; SSE consumer + client-side card renderer |\n| Phase 0 hosting | Hetzner server, local process | Simplest path; shared filesystem with NEEDLE workers |\n| Phase 1+ hosting | ardenone-cluster, k8s Deployment | Behind existing Traefik + Tailscale ingress |\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Phases\n\n### Phase 0 — Minimal Viable Surface (~2 days)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nValidates the core question: does routing + parallel dispatch reduce friction?\n\n- Single HTML page: textarea + mic button (Web Speech API)\n- `POST /dispatch` endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream\n- Results appear as basic cards as agents resolve\n- No persistence, no auth, no component library, no ambient push\n\nDeliverable: the core query loop working end-to-end.\n\n### Phase 1 — Session and Topics (~1 week)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nResults persist; the canvas has memory.\n\n- Session store (SQLite, 7+ tables with topic_context_cache and feedback_signals)\n- Topic model: canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place\n- Telegram surface fallback (reuse telegram-claude-bridge)\n- Bead watcher: closed NEEDLE beads push results to active surface\n- Workload summary on reconnect\n- Staleness indicators on cards\n\nDeliverable: sessions that survive browser refresh; Telegram fallback working.\n\n### Phase 2 — Self-Improvement Loop (~2 weeks)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nThe interface can be improved by talking into it.\n\n- Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n- Hot-reload confirmed at every artifact layer\n- Component library: UI-regen agent generates first components from actual result shapes\n- Canvas live-updates when a component is versioned\n- Explicit feedback processed: \"always include X\" → prompt updated → hot-reloaded\n\nDeliverable: at least one end-to-end self-modification cycle working (user instructs change, diff surfaced, approved, takes effect without redeploy).\n\n### Phase 3 — Responsiveness (~2-3 weeks)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nThe interface feels alive, not just reactive.\n\n- Ambient monitoring: active topics watched for state changes\n- Diff-aware results: show what changed since last result\n- Pre-warmed context: active topics refreshed in background every N minutes\n- Multi-turn within topic: follow-up questions deepen current topic context\n- Speculative pre-fetch: common follow-up patterns pre-fetched\n- Notification batching in audio mode\n- Implicit feedback signals fed to background analysis bead\n\nDeliverable: monitoring fires unprompted for a watched topic; follow-up questions are visibly faster.\n\n### Phase 4 — Audio Surface (~1-2 weeks)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nFull audio mode via Realtime API.\n\n- Realtime API voice session replaces text input path\n- Tool-as-trigger model: `dispatch_intent()` returns ack, result arrives async\n- Urgency-tiered voice narration\n- Audio-to-canvas session continuity\n\nDeliverable: full voice session with canvas catch-up on surface switch.\n\n### Future Work\n\n**Status: NOT STARTED** ❌\n\nPotential enhancements beyond Phase 4:\n- Multi-modal input (image processing for UI feedback via Agentation)\n- Advanced topic clustering and auto-archival\n- Cross-session context persistence with summarization\n- Mobile-native surface (iOS/Android app)\n- Collaborative sessions (multi-user shared canvases)\n- Advanced memory extraction with semantic search\n\n---\n\n## Self-Improvement: Hot-Reload Architecture\n\nEvery artifact that encodes behavior is readable, writable, and reloaded per-invocation:\n\n| Behavior | Artifact | Reload point |\n|----------|----------|-------------|\n| Utterance segmentation | `prompts/router.md` | Each router call |\n| Intent→project routing | `config/registry.yaml` | Each router call |\n| Context fetch strategy | `prompts/fetch/{type}.md` | Each fetch invocation |\n| Result format and detail | `prompts/synthesize.md` | Each synthesize call |\n| Voice narration style | `prompts/voice.md` | Each session turn |\n| Urgency classification | `prompts/urgency.md` | Each escalate call |\n| Visual rendering | Component library (DB) | Each card render |\n| Monitoring rules | `config/monitoring.yaml` | Each monitoring tick |\n\nPer-invocation reload is the implementation strategy (read file, check mtime). File watching adds complexity for no benefit — prompts change rarely and file reads are cheap.\n\n---\n\n## Surface Routing Rules\n\nWhen a result is ready to surface, the bead watcher or synthesize strand uses this priority:\n\n1. The surface the utterance originated from (if still connected)\n2. Most recently active connected surface\n3. Any connected surface\n4. Telegram (always-available fallback)\n\nException-class results (urgency: critical, type: exception) push to Telegram regardless of canvas state if no canvas has been active within the past N minutes.\n\n---\n\n## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n\naide-de-camp adds a routing and rendering layer on top of existing infrastructure without replacing any of it:\n\n- **NEEDLE workers** — unchanged. Task beads work exactly as before. The bead watcher is a new daemon that observes bead closure events.\n- **telegram-claude-bridge** — reused for Telegram surface and HUMAN bead push delivery.\n- **whisper-stt** — already deployed on ardenone-cluster; available as STT backend.\n- **ZAI proxy** — all direct API calls (router, synthesize, escalate) route through `llm-proxy.ardenone.com`.\n- **claude-governor** — governs subscription token consumption for self-modification and UI-regen task beads.\n- **DUCK-E** — FastAPI scaffolding, WebSocket handling, OpenAI Realtime API session management, and middleware reused as the voice layer foundation.\n- **beads (br CLI)** — task work items and HUMAN exceptions only. Conversational session state lives in a separate SQLite session store.\n- **kubectl proxies** — fetch strand uses existing kubectl proxy access per cluster.\n- **ArgoCD read-only proxy** — fetch strand reads ArgoCD application state via `argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444`.\n\nNet-new code: aide-de-camp is a substantial implementation spanning multiple subsystems. See the File System Layout section for the full module breakdown.\n\n---\n\n## Security Model\n\n- aide-de-camp runs on the Hetzner server; all cluster access via read-only kubectl proxies (same as existing tooling)\n- No cluster credentials stored by aide-de-camp; existing proxy infrastructure holds them\n- Self-modification agent writes only to the `prompts/` and `config/` directories within the repo\n- All artifact changes go through diff-review before application\n- Rollback available for any artifact via version history\n\n---\n\n## Open Questions\n\n1. **Routing accuracy** — how reliably can a haiku-class model split a rambling multi-project utterance into clean tagged threads? What's the false-split / under-split rate in practice?\n\n2. **Context latency** — can the fetch strand reliably hit kubectl + ArgoCD + git + beads within a 2-3s window? Which sources need timeouts and what's the degraded-result behavior?\n\n3. **Component selection** — when does the UI-regen agent generate a new component vs. stretch an existing one? How is \"good enough\" match defined?\n\n4. **Concurrency budget** — how many parallel synthesize calls can the ZAI proxy handle without queue pressure affecting the <3s target?\n\n5. **Topic vague reference resolution** — \"the pipeline\" vs. \"options pipeline\" vs. \"options-pipeline\". When does the router resolve from context vs. ask for clarification?\n\n6. **Voice UX** — push-to-talk vs. continuous listening + VAD silence detection. What's the right default for the audio surface?\n\n7. **Disambiguation flow** — when router confidence is below threshold, how does the clarification round-trip work without breaking conversational flow in audio mode?\n","numLines":789,"startLine":1,"totalLines":789}}}
